title: bumelia lanuginosa subsp. rigida
intro: Bumelia lanuginosa subsp. rigida, also known as the Texas redbud or fragrant bumelia, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Bumelia lanuginosa subsp. rigida include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve symptoms of colds and flu, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Bumelia lanuginosa subsp. rigida can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
